posts.author: Kathleenharris
posts.content: I got a similar dark spot (tear?) that you mention in my left eye last fall after driving up solo to Oregon for a week - though it moves around with the fluid in the eye like the floaters. It has not gone away but has become less bothersome as my brain appears to have adjusted to it and does not register it some of the time. I also have floaters that have been around for quite a while - again not always noticeable and depends on lighting conditions. Other than that I have no other vision issues except presbyopia which may be age-related (60) and I also blame intense blue-light abuse (ALAN) about 10-15 years ago. By the way, DH of a friend got a similar tear recently too. So this could be an early warning sign of the times! I have been doing facial ice-water dunks for a month now and see no significant change yet, but then again I am H1 haploytype and based on what Jack wrote in DM # 63 blog on eyes, I'm more susceptible to damage, and CT for photorepair is less effective for H1 compared to certain other haplotypes - though my eyes are brown so have more melanin than both my brothers. However, I am continuing every other day now with the CT but started DDW saline drops in my eyes first thing before I go out (just started a day or so ago). I do a lot of grounding and sun throughout the day but my environment sucks, so not expecting miracles till I move. I eat plenty of seafood and nuts and always use blueblockers when on laptop. I think being in ES will heal this issue, but jus…

posts.author: Jack Kruse
posts.content: PHYSICISTS JUST FOUND A BRAND-NEW WAY TO MAKE ELECTRONS ACT STRANGELY WITHOUT ANY MAGNETIC FIELD. In pentalayer graphene (five stacked and slightly twisted sheets), electrons slow down so dramatically that their mutual repulsion becomes the dominant force. The result? They form a collective quantum state that recreates the fractional quantum Hall effect but this time it’s “anomalous” (no external magnets needed). Why this matters: Normally this effect requires ultra-strong magnetic fields, ultra-clean materials, and temperatures near absolute zero. The moiré superlattice in twisted pentalayer graphene “fakes” the magnetic field from inside the material itself. This creates exotic anyons quasiparticles that behave as if they carry only a fraction of an electron’s charge. The deeper implication is staggering: Anyons are incredibly robust against noise and could be the key to building practical, fault-tolerant quantum computers that actually work at scale. We may have just unlocked a whole new playground for quantum materials one where the weirdest rules of quantum mechanics can be engineered on demand. What happens when we can routinely create and control these fractional-charge states in everyday lab conditions? The Solid-State Quantum Verification: Pentalayer Graphene is the Melanin- r-DNA Mirror The structural discovery of fractional quantum Hall states in twisted pentalayer graphene without any external magnetic field is the definitive, macro-scale material physics proof of…

posts.author: Mr.AJ
posts.content: Walk around the outside and see where it is coming from. Normally one side is the strongest, then look for towers. If no towers on that side close by good chance it is the neighbors WiFi. In single story buildings shielding the fence with wire mesh (metal window screen) can be a quick easy fix.

posts.author: Damir--G
posts.content: For a long time, I was unsure whether mineral water containing CO2 was actually healthy to drink or not. Most of the information I came across suggested that it might not be the healthiest option, but despite that, I continued drinking it because my intuition and the way I felt after drinking it were consistently positive. I always had a good feeling after drinking mineral water, so I trusted that feeling and kept it as part of my routine. I’m really glad that Jack explained the benefits of carbonated mineral water in several recent podcasts, because it made me feel much more reassured and confident about continuing to drink it.
